text: Hi everyone, do you know which are the highest paying engineers in the world?

then if there is a fresher who has just graduated,
his salary can be around 40 lakhs to 60 lakhs per annum.
And if there is an experienced professional in this field,
then his salary can go up to 2 crore plus per annum. Amazing, right?
And Quants engineers' dream companies are not FANG or MANG.
Their dream companies are Jane Street, Citadel, Tower Research, Jump Trading, etc.

a lot of interesting problems are being solved.

So first of all, let's know what a Quants engineer is.
A Quants engineer or quantitative developer is basically someone
who builds mathematical models and trading algorithms
for investment banks, trading firms, hedge funds, etc.
where data-driven trading decisions are taken.
Basically, this field of engineering is a mixture of software engineering,
mathematics, and statistics.
And in this field, you can expect tasks like
writing algorithms that run in microseconds based on market data,
optimizing execution speed,
backtesting strategies on massive data sets,
implementing risk models, etc.
Basically, these engineers mix maths, code, and finance
to make money faster than anyone.
And if you are wondering why Quants engineers are so highly paid,
then the simple reason is that they directly affect profit.
A better trading algorithm that runs faster
gives millions of dollars to a particular trading firm.
And because this trading algorithm is developed by Quants engineers,
that's why their salaries are so high.
Very few people have combined expertise in maths, finance, and programming.
So that's why competition is very tough for this role.
But equally, its rewards are very massive and lucrative.
Companies like Jane Street, Citadel, Tower Research, etc.
which I talked about in the beginning,
they give packages to freshers in India of around 60-80 lakhs per annum.
And globally, their packages range around $250,000+.
And if you become a Quants engineer,
then along with amazing packages and perks,
you get a chance to work on very interesting and intellectual problems.
You solve optimization problems,
analyze market behavior, make models on it,
write code that runs in microseconds and nanoseconds,
and you also get a chance to work with the world's smartest minds.
Amazing, right?
So if all these things excite you,
if all these things seem interesting to you,

And now let's discuss how to become a Quants engineer.
So the first step to becoming a Quants engineer is
to learn a programming language.
You have to master a particular programming language.
And the best and preferred programming language to become a Quants engineer
is C++.
Because it's the fastest.
C++ language is closely connected to the compiler.
And that's why its execution speed is very fast.
And that's why all the high-frequency trading firms,
investment banks, hedge funds,
to build algorithms in all of them,
this language is used.
So you should learn C++.
And the best resource to learn this language is C++ Primer Book.
This is a very good book.
And in this, your C++ concepts will be very clear.
So you can definitely check it out.
Then the second step is to master Maths and Probability.
This is the step where more than 80% of the people struggle.
Because solving the concepts of Maths and Probability becomes very difficult.
But you have to pay a lot of attention to this step.
And these particular 5 concepts should be absolutely clear to you.
And those are Probability, Statistics, Calculus, Optimization, and Linear Algebra.
Okay?
And the best resources to master these topics are
first one is Khan Academy,
which is a YouTube channel.
And it is the best resource to clear your basic concepts.
Then the second one is MIT Open Course.
MIT, which is the world's top engineering university,
has a course by the name of Introduction to Probability.
You can definitely check it out.
And finally, there is an amazing book called
Introduction to Probability by Blitzstein and Huang.
And you can definitely check out this book.
And with the help of all these resources,
you can clearly clear your concepts of Maths and Probability.
Now the next step is to understand Finance and Trading Concepts.
Now because you want to become a Quants Engineer,
you should have some knowledge of Quants.
So you should know the basics of Finance and Trading.
You don't need to be an expert.
But yes, you should definitely have basic knowledge.
So you should know about Stocks, Options, Futures, etc.
You should know about Market Microstructure,
what is Arbitrage,
how are Trades executed, etc.
To master all these things,
you can refer a YouTube channel by the name of Aswath Damodaran.

And it will help you to improve your knowledge of Finance.
And along with this, there is a book by the name of
Options, Futures, and Other Derivatives by John C. Hull.
You can also refer this book to improve your knowledge of Finance.
Then comes the next step which is actually the most important step.
And that is Data Analysis and Machine Learning.
Quants Engineers also use different Machine Learning models

Fast.ai and Course Era's Machine Learning course by Andrew.

and that is that Quant interviews are tough
but they are actually very predictable.
In Quant interviews,
you will be tested on only three things.
Maths, Coding, and Finance.
In Maths, you will be asked about
Probability, Combitronics, Puzzles, etc.
In Coding, you will be asked about DSA.
And in Finance, you will be asked about logic-based questions.
For example,
Math related question can be
What is the expected number of coin tosses to get two heads?
Coding related question can be
Implement an order book in C++.
And Finance related question can be
Explain Risk Modeling, Volatility, etc.
So you have to prepare all these things
and the best resource to master them
that is a book called Hurt on the Street by Timothy Crack.
This is a very amazing book to prepare for Quant interviews.
And you can definitely check it out.
Then to master Coding,
you have to solve medium to hard level questions on LeetCode
and have to give different contests.
And finally, you can give mock interviews
on Pramp and interviewing.io.

your preparation for the interview will be amazing.
